# Lyra Documentation

Site: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/
Repository: https://github.com/RodolfoFigueroa/lyra

Lyra is a FastAPI service for metric execution through schema v3 plugin manifests, Redis-backed jobs, and warm queue-specific Celery workers.

## Primary Docs

- Overview: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/
- Getting Started: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/getting-started/
- Architecture: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/architecture/
- Contributor Guide: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/contributor-guide/
- Local Development: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/local-development/
- Testing And Quality: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/testing-and-quality/
- Releases: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/releases/
- Plugin Quickstart: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/plugin-quickstart/
- Plugin Author Checklist: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/plugin-author-checklist/
- Plugin Manifests: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/plugin-manifests/
- Spatial Plugin Inputs: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/spatial-plugin-inputs/
- Runner Plugins: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/runner-plugins/
- lyra-sdk: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/lyra-sdk/
- lyra-api: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/lyra-api/
- lyra-utils: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/lyra-utils/
- API Reference: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/api-reference/
- Job API: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/job-api/
- Metrics Catalog: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/metrics-catalog/
- Python Client: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/python-client/
- Deployment: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/deployment/
- Operations: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/operations/
- Reference: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/reference/
- AI Agent Guide: https://rodolfofigueroa.github.io/lyra/ai-agent-guide/

## Current Contracts

- Plugin authoring: `PluginDefinition`, `LocationInput`, `BoundsInput`, `Batch`, and `BatchItem` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/plugin.py`; `lyra-plugin build-manifest` generates `PluginManifestV3`
- Job models: `JobCreateRequest`, `JobCreateResponse`, `JobEnvelope`, `JobEvent`, `TableJobResult`, `FileJobResult`, `FailedJobResult`, `CancelledJobResult`, `TerminalJobResult`, `JobStatusInfo`, `JobListResponse`, `JobCancelResponse` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/job.py`
- Runner context: `RunContext` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/context.py`
- Database context: `LyraDB` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/db.py`, available as optional `RunContext.db`
- Explicit spatial inputs: `ExplicitLocationAPI` and `ExplicitBoundsAPI` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/types.py`
- Geometry models: `GeoJSON` and `SingleGeoJSON` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/geometry.py`
- Data type schema models: `DataTypeSchemaInfo` and `DataTypesResponse` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/data_types.py`
- Admin operation models: plugin repo, catalog refresh, worker restart, and plugin routing contracts in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/admin.py`
- Lookup models: `MetZoneCodeResponse` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/lookups.py`
- Observability models: `LivenessResponse`, `ReadinessResponse`, `AdminStatusResponse`, `ConfigSummaryResponse`, `CatalogSummaryResponse`, `WorkersResponse`, `WorkerDetail`, and `QueuesResponse` in `packages/lyra_sdk/src/lyra/sdk/models/observability.py`
- Python clients: `LyraAPIClient` and `AsyncLyraAPIClient` in `packages/lyra_api/src/lyra/api/`
- Plugin helpers: GeoJSON conversion, date ranges, and Earth Engine utilities in `packages/lyra_utils/src/lyra/utils/`
- Generated Python API reference: `docs/src/content/docs/api-reference/`, created by `npm run generate:api --prefix docs`
- Job routes: `lyra_app/routes/jobs.py`
- Catalog: `lyra_app/registry.py`
- Worker: `lyra_app/worker.py`
- Job store: `lyra_app/job_store.py`

## Public Routes

- `GET /data-types` returns grouped `location` and `bounds` wrapper schemas
- `GET /live`
- `GET /ready`
- `GET /metrics`
- `GET /metrics/{metric_name}`
- `POST /jobs`
- `GET /jobs/{job_id}`
- `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events`
- `GET /jobs/{job_id}/result`
- `GET /jobs/{job_id}/result/download`
- `GET /lookups/met-zones`
- `GET /admin/plugin-repos`
- `POST /admin/plugin-repos`
- `PATCH /admin/plugin-repos/{repo_id}`
- `DELETE /admin/plugin-repos/{repo_id}`
- `POST /admin/plugin-repos/{repo_id}/sync`
- `POST /admin/plugin-catalog/refresh`
- `POST /admin/workers/restart`
- `GET /admin/status`
- `GET /admin/config-summary`
- `GET /admin/catalog`
- `GET /admin/workers`
- `GET /admin/workers/{worker_name}`
- `GET /admin/queues`
- `GET /admin/jobs`
- `POST /admin/jobs/{job_id}/cancel`
- `GET /admin/plugin-routing`
- `PUT /admin/plugin-routing/{metric_name}`
- `DELETE /admin/plugin-routing/{metric_name}`

## Agent Notes

- Metrics are not built into the app. They come from plugin sources stored in `/lyra_data/state/plugins.toml` and managed through `/admin/plugin-repos`, including GitHub entries, explicit `file://` local git repositories, and development `dir://` directory snapshots. Metric queue assignments are stored with the repo that exposes each metric and are removed when that repo is deleted.
- Use `/metrics` to discover metric names and JSON Schemas before submitting jobs.
- Use the Python Client page for end-to-end `lyra-api` workflows, and the lyra-api page for constructor, method, and exception reference.
- For spatial plugins, use the Spatial Plugin Inputs page. Decorated functions declare `LocationInput` or `BoundsInput`; the API validates wrapper payloads, resolves them, and the registry passes parsed geometry models to the function.
- Public metric execution uses the `lyra.run_metric` Celery task internally.
- API containers read generated manifests. Worker containers install plugin code, import its `PluginDefinition`, and reject stale manifest artifacts.
